name: Stubb's Waller Creek Amphitheater
parking: No dedicated lot. Use street meters (free after 6 PM/12 AM) or Hotel Indigo/Holiday Inn Express Garage at 9th & Red River. Rideshare recommended.
age_limit: Generally All Ages (under 17 needs guardian). Some shows 18+ or 21+.
vibe: Quintessential Austin. Dusty, sloped outdoor dirt floor (wear boots!). Sloped ground means great sightlines even from the back. Famous BBQ attached.
tips: Cashless venue. Outdoor show, check weather.

name: Mohawk
parking: Very limited street parking on Red River. Use St. David’s Church Garage or paid lots south of 6th St.
age_limit: Varies. Many All Ages, but late-night events often 21+. Check ticket link.
vibe: Indie heaven. Multi-level with indoor and outdoor stages. Balconies offer the best views in the city. Raw, authentic, and loud.
tips: Cashless venue. Check both inside and outside stage schedules.

name: Emo's Austin
parking: Do NOT park in adjacent shopping center lots (instant tow). Use street parking on Royal Crest Dr or Burton Dr.
age_limit: Usually All Ages. Strict ID checks for 21+ wristbands.
vibe: Professional black-box warehouse. Standing room only, big-tour feel, top-tier sound system.
tips: Cashless venue. High energy, can get very crowded.

name: Moody Center
parking: Expensive ($40-$60). Pre-book Manor or Trinity Garage. Bullock Museum lot is a cheaper 15-min walk away.
vibe: Modern, massive (15k capacity), air-conditioned. Incredible acoustics. Local food vendors like H-E-B and Stubb's inside.
tips: Cashless venue. Bag policy is strict (clear bags only).

name: Empire Control Room & Garage
parking: Street parking on E. 7th or nearby paid lots. Rideshare easier due to one-way streets.
vibe: Industrial/Experimental. Intimate 'Control Room' with immersive visuals + open-air 'Garage'. Go-to for electronic, hip-hop, and psych-rock.

name: Scoot Inn
parking: Limited street parking. Official garage at 501 Navasota St (one block north).
vibe: Established 1871. Cozy, string-lit backyard party vibe. Great for Americana, indie, and folk.
tips: Cashless venue. Outdoor venue, very relaxed atmosphere. FOOD: Usually has a resident food truck (like Arlo's) on the patio.

name: The Ballroom at Spider House
parking: Aggressive towing area! Small lot behind venue fills fast. Street parking on W 29th/30th is resident-only/permit heavy. Rideshare highly recommended.
age_limit: Usually 18+ or 21+. Strictly enforced ID policy.
vibe: Eclectic 'Old Austin'. 1920s bungalow meets punk dive bar. Intimate indoor stage with retro decor and a large breezy patio shared with Tweedy's Bar.
tips: Check out the food trucks on the patio. Extremely casual dress code. Arrive early if you want a table inside.

name: Antone's Nightclub
parking: Street parking or nearby downtown garages. Valet often available at Westin/Eddie V's.
vibe: The 'Home of the Blues'. Historic, polished downtown spot with legendary sound and a welcoming atmosphere.
tips: Gift shop on site. Legendary Austin history.

name: Hole in the Wall
parking: Limited street parking on Guadalupe. Side streets nueces/Rio Grande free after 6 PM. Rear lot free for patrons AFTER 9 PM.
age_limit: Usually 21+ after 9 PM. Happy hours/daytime often all-ages.
vibe: Classic gritty UT-area dive. Gritty, authentic, and a rite of passage for local indie bands.
tips: Casual dress code. Right across from UT campus.

name: Continental Club
parking: Very difficult street parking on South Congress. Rideshare or bus recommended.
vibe: Dimly lit, 1950s hot-rod/rockabilly aesthetic. Intimate and steeped in roots/country/blues history.
tips: Upstairs 'Gallery' has a separate calendar and a quieter vibe.

name: ACL Live at The Moody Theater
parking: Paid garages at the W Hotel or Austin City Hall. Metered street parking is rare.
vibe: Pristine sound and sightlines. State-of-the-art facility where Austin City Limits is filmed. Polished and professional.
tips: Seated venue for many shows, check your ticket for floor vs. balcony.

name: The White Horse
parking: Small lot available, but mostly residential street parking. Check signs carefully.
vibe: High-energy hipster honky-tonk. Always crowded, always dancing. Expect country, bluegrass, and a lot of whiskey.
tips: They have a great outdoor taco truck and a pool table.

name: Broken Spoke
parking: Dedicated lot on-site, but it fills up very early.
vibe: World-famous honky-tonk established 1864. True Texas grit, chicken fried steak, and two-stepping lessons.
tips: Get there early for dance lessons if you're a beginner.

name: Saxon Pub
parking: Small dedicated lot and street parking. Lot fills fast.
vibe: Legendary listening room. Intimate, respectful crowd, focus is 100% on the songwriter.
tips: Known for the neon sign and incredible acoustics.

name: Moody Amphitheater at Waterloo Park
parking: Paid garages nearby (Waterloo Greenway). No on-site parking.
vibe: Scenic outdoor park stage with Austin skyline backdrop. New and modern with great production.
tips: Lawn seating is common, bring a blanket if allowed for the show.

name: Cactus Cafe
parking: Paid UT campus garages (San Antonio St). Very little street parking.
vibe: Historic, world-class acoustic listening room inside the UT Union building. Legendary for folk and Americana.
tips: Arrive early; seating is very limited and intimate.
